hud.by: BY FELIPE CORREDOR CASTRO // Senior Architect

arsenal.description: My commitment to the tech community and software engineering. Here you can audit the architecture, code quality, and impact (stars and views) of my {count} projects, dynamically synced with GitHub.
arsenal.default_description: Explore the technical architecture and source code of this node directly on GitHub.

omnicon.title: Omnicon - Industrial Digital Transformation
omnicon.budget: $2,500 - $3,500 USD per month (~$12,000 USD MVP total)
omnicon.clientOverview: Global Systems Integrator for industrial automation. They required an internal management platform to optimize project execution and data handling in heavy engineering sectors such as Oil, Gas, and Pharmaceutical.
results: Highly intuitive platform for complex industrial workflows
Successful transformation from manual tracking to digital visibility
Positive reception from internal engineering stakeholders
Standardized digital workflow across active global projects

clientFeedback.quote: When we talk with the tech lead, he demonstrates that he understands what we need and adds value to our process.

faq.question: How does digital transformation impact project management in engineering sectors?
faq.answer: It shifts the focus from manual tracking to real-time visibility. By automating resource allocation and progress reporting, engineering teams can focus on problem-solving rather than administrative overhead.

faq.question: What are the common challenges when developing internal tools for industrial companies?
faq.answer: Industrial companies handle massive data silos and legacy workflows. The challenge lies in creating a unified interface that translates complex shop-floor data into actionable business intelligence.

elephant-cpa.title: Elephant CPA - SaaS Management Solution
elephant-cpa.clientOverview: ElephantCPA, a dynamic platform designed to streamline interactions between accountants and their clients. They had a Minimum Viable Product (MVP) but sought to elevate their platform in terms of functionality, stability, and cybersecurity. A cloud-based practice management software solution for small to medium-sized accounting firms in the United States, they needed to move from an MVP to a scalable solution with enhanced security.
results: Implementation of over 130 user stories
80% reduction in cloud costs
Enhanced cybersecurity and reduced exposure
Product that exceeded user expectations
Seamless experience for accountants and clients
Rapid problem resolution (crisis management)
Bi-weekly agile delivery schedule

clientFeedback.quote: The engineering team is transparent and they are very honest with the work they perform.

faq.question: What are the signs that a development house is a 'feature factory' vs. a strategic product development partner?
faq.answer: Feature factories take orders, say yes to everything, avoid asking questions, and focus only on output volume. Strategic partners question assumptions, suggest better workflows, prioritize based on impact, and contribute product thinking, not just coding.

faq.question: What is the real cost difference between building accounting software with a nearshore team vs. a US-based team?
faq.answer: Nearshore teams in Latin America typically cost between $35 and $65 USD/hour for senior profiles, compared to $120-$180 USD/hour in the US. A 7-month MVP could cost between $180k and $300k USD nearshore vs. $500k-$900k USD in the US for comparable quality.

faq.question: How do I verify if a 'senior full-stack developer' is not actually a mid-level with an inflated title?
faq.answer: We review their contributions in complex repositories, ask deep architectural questions during technical interviews, and verify if they truly make technical decisions or simply follow tickets.

faq.question: Should I build custom accounting software or use existing platforms like Karbon?
faq.answer: If your workflow is standard, commercial solutions are more cost-effective. If your firm has unique processes, proprietary automation logic, or seeks differentiating capabilities, custom-built is the best long-term investment.

faq.question: What happens if the development team fails on deadlines or delivers poor quality code?
faq.answer: A strategic partner offers guarantees and clear escalation processes. In our case, we implement bi-weekly demos to detect issues early and maintain a 'crisis management' protocol for immediate resolution.

2moon-capital.title: 2Moon Capital - Algorithmic Trading Solution
2moon-capital.clientOverview: 2Moon required a sophisticated solution to manage and execute complex trading strategies efficiently. The project focused on creating an automated, reliable, and scalable trading system that could respond dynamically to market signals and manage multiple trading strategies and Interactive Brokers accounts effectively.
results: Automated trading system powered by the specialized 'Joshua' library
High-performance serverless architecture on AWS
Operational multi-strategy account management
Three paper trading environments + one real money environment
Effective risk management through automated closing conditions

faq.question: What are the signs that a development company doesn't understand trading systems?
faq.answer: Red flags include vague answers about market data, promises of fixed timelines for strategy performance, failure to mention risk controls, misuse of backtesting vs. live trading terminology, and lack of experience with broker APIs like IBKR or FIX.

faq.question: What testing environment costs should I budget for simulated trading?
faq.answer: While simulated trading accounts are often free, cloud infrastructure (AWS) and real-time market data sources can cost between $200 and $1,000 USD monthly depending on data volume.

faq.question: How many iterations should I expect before having a production-ready trading system?
faq.answer: Usually three to eight iterations: initial prototype, paper trading, real-time simulation, limited capital testing, and then full deployment with monitoring.

faq.question: What happens to the intellectual property of my trading algorithms?
faq.answer: In our model, the client owns 100% of the intellectual property from day one. The business value resides in the proprietary logic of the algorithms, not just the execution platform.

faq.question: How much does it cost to build an algorithmic trading platform?
faq.answer: A custom trading platform typically ranges between $80k and $450k USD depending on complexity, order types, latency requirements, data integrations, and security needs.

canadian-orthodontic-partners.title: Canadian Orthodontic Partners & Docbraces
canadian-orthodontic-partners.clientOverview: A network of over 50 orthodontic clinics across Canada. They needed to bridge gaps between consumer-facing websites and CRM systems (HubSpot), and develop digital solutions for secure contract creation and treatment option presentations for patients.
results: Generated approximately $2 million in revenue through the payment calculator tool
Saved thousands of person-hours through digital contract signing
Increased visibility across more than 50 clinics simultaneously
Secure collection of sensitive patient information
User-friendly transition from complex Excel to optimized Web App

clientFeedback.quote: The engineering team's greatest strengths are their dedication and affordable rates.

faq.question: What is the key to successfully connecting a website and a CRM like HubSpot?
faq.answer: The key is data integrity and real-time synchronization. We use custom API middleware to ensure that patient leads from the site map correctly to HubSpot properties, preventing data loss and allowing automated follow-ups.

faq.question: How do you ensure security when handling patient contracts digitally?
faq.answer: We implement end-to-end encryption for document storage and use secure authenticated links for digital signatures, complying with health privacy standards (PIPEDA in Canada).

faq.question: What is the real timeline from contract signing to MVP launch?
faq.answer: For an enterprise tool like this, an MVP typically takes 12 to 16 weeks. This includes discovery phases, UI/UX design, development in 2-week sprints, and a robust testing phase with real clinic coordinators.

hello-leena.title: Hello Leena - Telemedicine EMR Platform
hello-leena.clientOverview: A telemedicine provider in the mental health space (BIPOC focused). The project involved building a complete end-to-end Electronic Medical Record (EMR) system to manage patient data, telemedicine sessions, and complex business reporting.
results: Successful delivery of a production-ready EMR system
Automated reporting for business management
Seamless integration of secure payments and telemedicine video
High-velocity sprint delivery monitored in ClickUp
Great acclaim from internal stakeholders for the organizational impact of the system

clientFeedback.quote: The engineering team is incredible for the way they learn about your business and do everything possible to deliver beyond expectations.

faq.question: Why build a custom EMR instead of using a standard health SaaS?
faq.answer: Mental health clinics often have unique flows for matching therapists with patients based on their identity (BIPOC). A custom EMR allows for specific matching algorithms, proprietary reports, and total control over the client experience.

faq.question: How do you handle security for telemedicine video sessions?
faq.answer: We use HIPAA-compliant video SDKs with end-to-end encryption, ensuring that therapy sessions are never recorded or stored on intermediate servers without explicit authorization.

faq.question: Should I hire a full team or start with a single developer for MVP validation?
faq.answer: For an EMR, you need a multidisciplinary team (Frontend, Backend, Security, Design) from day one. Starting with a single developer often leads to architectural shortcuts that turn into massive technical debt when handling real patient data.

fruto-salvaje.title: Fruto Salvaje - E-Commerce Empire
fruto-salvaje.clientOverview: A massive beauty and personal care brand scaling to international traffic. The platform requires high-conversion engineering, ritualistic content integration (Moon Phases), and global performance optimization.
results: Successful management of over 190 high-engagement reviews
Seamless integration of community rituals into the purchase flow
Optimized global load times for high-traffic sessions
Unified brand identity across all digital touchpoints

clientFeedback.quote: Excellent product, I love it! The site makes shopping very easy.

faq.question: What is the key to scaling a local beauty brand to a global Shopify store?
faq.answer: Performance and localization. Ensuring the site loads in less than 2 seconds globally while supporting multiple currencies and languages is critical to maintaining the premium feel.

faq.question: How do you integrate unconventional features like 'Moon Phases' into e-commerce?
faq.answer: We treat ritualistic data as dynamic content entities, mapping these phases to specific product recommendations to drive cross-selling without interrupting the funnel.
